titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Karla Kuskin Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday February 28, 2020 at 04:01

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.physdesc: (67 boxes) + 15 broadside, 5 rolls, 1 record album storage

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Box 30 (audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile material. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 33 (born digital): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 86 (record album storage): Restricted fragile material. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Glenn Horowitz Bookseller, Inc. on the Jockey Hollow Fund, 2010. Gift of the Estate of Karla Kuskin, 2010, and gift of Julia Kuskin, 2017.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into twelve series: I. Writings, circa 1930s-2009. II. Correspondence, circa 1920s-2000s. III. Journals, 1934 - 2003. IV. Artwork, 1956 - 2008. V. Photographs, circa 1930s-1990s. VI. Business papers, circa 1960s-2000s. VII. Personal papers, circa 1880s-1990s. VIII. Awards and objects, circa 1979-2005. IX. Audiovisual materials, undated. X. Born digital materials, undated. XI. June 2017 acquisition, circa 1950s. XII. August 2017 acquisition, circa 1950-1953, undated.

bioghist.head: Karla Kuskin
bioghist.p: Karla Kuskin (1932-2009) was a writer and artist, most well-known for her works of children's literature both as an author and an illustrator. Born in New York, NY, Kuskin attended Yale University, graduating with a B.F.A in graphic design in 1955. Over the course of her career, Kuskin wrote over fifty books including "Roar and More", "ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ", and "The Rose on My Cake".

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Karla Kuskin Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Karla Kuskin Papers, which span from 1880 to 2009, contain writings and artwork, along with business and personal papers related to Kuskin's life and work. The papers also contain correspondence, reviews, photographs, audiovisual materials, and born digital materials.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Karla Kuskin Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials within this series maintain the original physical organization found at time of acquisition with the exception of some oversized materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains writings, drafts, journals, and other materials related to Kuskin's creative work.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains unpublished stories, poems, and verses for music by Karla Kuskin. Titles include: "Rodeo Riders" and "Quong". Also includes material written by both her father and mother, Sidney T. Siedman and Mitzi Salzman Siedman, along with index cards with speech notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains speeches and manuscript material. Titles include: "The Flying Pig and Other Winged Creatures", "The Pittsburg Steelers Go To Bed", "Space Story", "Channukah", "Sing For Joy", "The Man Who Nevered Snoozed", "Xingu", "ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ", "Jerusalem Shinning Still", "Rose Oh", "The Bear Who Saw the Spring", "Which Horse Is William?", "The Philharmonic Gets Dressed", "The Sky Is Always in the Sky", "James and the Rain", "I Am Me", "Paul", "Near the Window Tree", and "What Did You Bring Me".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains speeches along with published and unpublished writings. Also contains reviews and Maurice Sendak materials.

did.unittitle: Proofs of "What Is It Like To Be a Cat"
Any Me I Want to Be
The Bear Who Saw the Spring
A Boy Had a Mother Who Bought Him a Hat
June Spoon and Her Very Adventurous Search for the Moon
In the Middle of the Trees
The Walk the Mouse Girls Took
Watson, the Smartest Dog in the U.S.A.
Watson, the Smartest Dog in the U.S.A.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials within this series maintain the original physical organization found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains extensive correspondence between Kuskin and her family, along with other letters and cards from friends.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence from Mark Strand.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters from school children (circa 1980s-1990s), faxes, and correspondence with Norman Schwatz (circa 1990s).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence with Sidney T. Siedman and Mitzi Salzman Siedman, as well as fan letters, work-related correspondence and letters from friends. Also contains a Karla Kuskin memorial booklet.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence with Karla Kuskin's family, primarily her parents: Sidney T. Siedman and Mitzi Salzman Siedman.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence with Karla Kuskin's family, primarily her parents: Sidney T. Siedman and Mitzi Salzman Siedman.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence with Karla Kuskin's family, primarily her parents: Sidney T. Siedman and Mitzi Salzman Siedman.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence with Karla Kuskin's family, primarily her parents: Sidney T. Siedman and Mitzi Salzman Siedman.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains fan letters, business correspondence, correspendence between friends, as well as letters between Karla Kuskin's grandparents and her parents.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence and telegrams with Karla Kuskin's family, as well letters with Stanley David and Charles Kuskin.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials within this series maintain the original physical organization found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains journals, datebooks, and notebooks used by Kuskin during her lifetime.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also contains Mitzi Salzman Siedman's journal (circa 1934-1982).

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials within this series maintain the original physical organization found at time of acquisition with the exception of some oversized materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains sketches, drawings, and other artwork related to Kuskin's creative output and her work as a children's book illustrator.

did.unittitle: Artwork related to "Alexander Soames" and "How Do You Get from Here to There?"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains raw materials and photocopies used for artwork and homemade cards. Also includes some desk materials.

did.unittitle: Dogs and Dragons, Trees and Dreams
Any Me I Want to Be
The Bear Who Saw the Spring
A Boy Had a Mother Who Bought Him a Hat
The Dog That Lost His Family
How Do You Get from Here to There?
In the Middle of the Trees
The Walk the Mouse Girls Took
Watson, the Smartest Dog in the U.S.A.--mechanicals
Watson, the Smartest Dog in the U.S.A.--mechanicals
What Shall We Do and Allee Galloo!

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials within this series maintain the original physical organization found at time of acquisition.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials within this series maintain the original physical organization found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains business papers, reviews, and other publicity materials related to Kuskin's work.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains reviews, biographical material, and publicity specifically related to Karla Kuskin's works.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains reviews and magazines, along with other business paper including contracts, permissions, and publishing correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains business and and personal papers related to Karla Kuskin and her works. Titles of writings include: "Alexander Soames", "The Animals and the Ark", "City Dog", "Any Me I Want to Be", "Dogs and Dragons, Trees and Dreams", "I Am Me", "A Great Miracle Happened There: A Chanukah Story", "Moon, Have You Met My Mother?", "The Sky Is Always in the Sky", "So, What's It Like to Be a Cat?", "Something about the Author Autobiography Series", "Square as a House", "Toots the Cat", "The Walk the Mouse Girls Took", "Under My Hood I Have a Hat", "Which Horse Is William?", "The Bear Who Saw the Spring", and others.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials within this series maintain the original physical organization found at time of acquisition with the exception of some oversized materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains Kuskin's personal papers including posters and some artwork.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains personal papers including family papers (birth, death and marriage certificates), mementos, and homemade cards.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains personal papers including seasonal cards, autobiographical materials, and Karla Kuskin's school papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains posters for The Downtown Balitmore Children's Centers.

did.unittitle: Poetry in Motion subway posters--"The Question"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes posters for Jerusalem Still Shining and The Dorian Woodwind Quintet.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials within this series maintain the original physical organization found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains awards and certificates given to Kuskin, along with ink rollers used in the creation of Kuskin's artwork.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Poetry Parade: Poets Read Their Poetry For Children records

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Photograph negatives and photographic prints within this series are arranged in the original physical organization found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photograph negatives of Karla Kuskin at Yale and New York City, as well as photographic prints.

did.unittitle: Black and white negatives and photographic prints

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographic prints and black and white negatives by Karla Kuskin.

did.unittitle: Photographic prints, black and white negatives, and transparencies
